109	class Flask(App):
110	    """The flask object implements a WSGI application and acts as the central
111	    object.  It is passed the name of the module or package of the
112	    application.  Once it is created it will act as a central registry for
113	    the view functions, the URL rules, template configuration and much more.
114	
115	    The name of the package is used to resolve resources from inside the
116	    package or the folder the module is contained in depending on if the
117	    package parameter resolves to an actual python package (a folder with
118	    an :file:`__init__.py` file inside) or a standard module (just a ``.py`` file).
119	
120	    For more information about resource loading, see :func:`open_resource`.
121	
122	    Usually you create a :class:`Flask` instance in your main module or
123	    in the :file:`__init__.py` file of your package like this::
124	
125	        from flask import Flask
126	        app = Flask(__name__)
127	
128	    .. admonition:: About the First Parameter
129	
130	        The idea of the first parameter is to give Flask an idea of what
131	        belongs to your application.  This name is used to find resources
132	        on the filesystem, can be used by extensions to improve debugging
133	        information and a lot more.
134	
135	        So it's important what you provide there.  If you are using a single
136	        module, `__name__` is always the correct value.  If you however are
137	        using a package, it's usually recommended to hardcode the name of
138	        your package there.
139	
140	        For example if your application is defined in :file:`yourapplication/app.py`
141	        you should create it with one of the two versions below::
142	
143	            app = Flask('yourapplication')
144	            app = Flask(__name__.split('.')[0])
145	
146	        Why is that?  The application will work even with `__name__`, thanks
147	        to how resources are looked up.  However it will make debugging more
148	        painful.  Certain extensions can make assumptions based on the
149	        import name of your application.  For example the Flask-SQLAlchemy
150	        extension will look for the code in your application that triggered
151	        an SQL query in debug mode.  If the import name is not properly set
152	        up, that debugging information is lost.  (For example it would only
153	        pick up SQL queries in `yourapplication.app` and not
154	        `yourapplication.views.frontend`)
155	
156	    .. versionadded:: 0.7
157	       The `static_url_path`, `static_folder`, and `template_folder`
158	       parameters were added.

254	    def __init_subclass__(cls, **kwargs: t.Any) -> None:
255	        import warnings

257	        # These method signatures were updated to take a ctx param. Detect
258	        # overridden methods in subclasses that still have the old signature.
259	        # Show a deprecation warning and wrap to call with correct args.
260	        for method in (
261	            cls.handle_http_exception,
262	            cls.handle_user_exception,
263	            cls.handle_exception,
264	            cls.log_exception,
265	            cls.dispatch_request,
266	            cls.full_dispatch_request,
267	            cls.finalize_request,
268	            cls.make_default_options_response,
269	            cls.preprocess_request,
270	            cls.process_response,
271	            cls.do_teardown_request,
272	            cls.do_teardown_appcontext,
273	        ):
274	            base_method = getattr(Flask, method.__name__)
275	
276	            if method is base_method:
277	                # not overridden
278	                continue
279	
280	            # get the second parameter (first is self)
281	            iter_params = iter(inspect.signature(method).parameters.values())
282	            next(iter_params)
283	            param = next(iter_params, None)
284	
285	            # must have second parameter named ctx or annotated AppContext
286	            if param is None or not (
287	                # no annotation, match name
288	                (param.annotation is inspect.Parameter.empty and param.name == "ctx")
289	                or (

291	                    isinstance(param.annotation, str)
292	                    and param.annotation.rpartition(".")[2] == "AppContext"
293	                )
294	                or (

296	                    inspect.isclass(param.annotation)
297	                    and issubclass(param.annotation, AppContext)
298	                )
299	            ):
300	                warnings.warn(

302	                    " as the first parameter. The old signature is deprecated"
303	                    " and will not be supported in Flask 4.0.",
304	                    DeprecationWarning,
305	                    stacklevel=2,

417	        """Open a resource file relative to :attr:`root_path` for reading.
418	
419	        For example, if the file ``schema.sql`` is next to the file
420	        ``app.py`` where the ``Flask`` app is defined, it can be opened
421	        with:

424	
425	            with app.open_resource("schema.sql") as f:
426	                conn.executescript(f.read())
427

429	        :param mode: Open the file in this mode. Only reading is supported,
430	            valid values are ``"r"`` (or ``"rt"``) and ``"rb"``.
431	        :param encoding: Open the file with this encoding when opening in text
432	            mode. This is ignored when opening in binary mode.
433	
434	        .. versionchanged:: 3.1
435	            Added the ``encoding`` parameter.
436	        """
437	        if mode not in {"r", "rt", "rb"}:
438	            raise ValueError("Resources can only be opened for reading.")

441	
442	        if mode == "rb":
443	            return open(path, mode)  # pyright: ignore

512	        up so the request is passed explicitly.

516	            only that domain, for both ``subdomain_matching`` and
517	            ``host_matching``.

534	            request.host = get_host(request.environ, request.trusted_hosts)  # pyright: ignore

590	    def update_template_context(
591	        self, ctx: AppContext, context: dict[str, t.Any]
592	    ) -> None:
593	        """Update the template context with some commonly used variables.
594	        This injects request, session, config and g into the template
595	        context as well as everything template context processors want
596	        to inject.  Note that the as of Flask 0.6, the original values
597	        in the context will not be overridden if a context processor
598	        decides to return a value with the same key.
599	
600	        :param context: the context as a dictionary that is updated in place
601	                        to add extra variables.
602	        """
603	        names: t.Iterable[str | None] = (None,)
604	
605	        # A template may be rendered outside a request context.
606	        if ctx.has_request:
607	            names = chain(names, reversed(ctx.request.blueprints))
608

610	        # copy to re-apply after all context functions.
611	        orig_ctx = context.copy()
612	
613	        for name in names:
614	            if name in self.template_context_processors:
615	                for func in self.template_context_processors[name]:
616	                    context.update(self.ensure_sync(func)())

618	        context.update(orig_ctx)

632	    def run(
633	        self,
634	        host: str | None = None,
635	        port: int | None = None,
636	        debug: bool | None = None,
637	        load_dotenv: bool = True,
638	        **options: t.Any,
639	    ) -> None:
640	        """Runs the application on a local development server.
641	
642	        Do not use ``run()`` in a production setting. It is not intended to
643	        meet security and performance requirements for a production server.
644	        Instead, see :doc:`/deploying/index` for WSGI server recommendations.
645	
646	        If the :attr:`debug` flag is set the server will automatically reload
647	        for code changes and show a debugger in case an exception happened.
648	
649	        If you want to run the application in debug mode, but disable the
650	        code execution on the interactive debugger, you can pass
651	        ``use_evalex=False`` as parameter.  This will keep the debugger's
652	        traceback screen active, but disable code execution.
653	
654	        It is not recommended to use this function for development with
655	        automatic reloading as this is badly supported.  Instead you should
656	        be using the :command:`flask` command line script's ``run`` support.
657	
658	        .. admonition:: Keep in Mind
659	
660	           Flask will suppress any server error with a generic error page
661	           unless it is in debug mode.  As such to enable just the
662	           interactive debugger without the code reloading, you have to
663	           invoke :meth:`run` with ``debug=True`` and ``use_reloader=False``.
664	           Setting ``use_debugger`` to ``True`` without being in debug mode
665	           won't catch any exceptions because there won't be any to
666	           catch.
667	
668	        :param host: the hostname to listen on. Set this to ``'0.0.0.0'`` to
669	            have the server available externally as well. Defaults to
670	            ``'127.0.0.1'`` or the host in the ``SERVER_NAME`` config variable
671	            if present.
672	        :param port: the port of the webserver. Defaults to ``5000`` or the
673	            port defined in the ``SERVER_NAME`` config variable if present.
674	        :param debug: if given, enable or disable debug mode. See
675	            :attr:`debug`.
676	        :param load_dotenv: Load the nearest :file:`.env` and :file:`.flaskenv`
677	            files to set environment variables. Will also change the working
678	            directory to the directory containing the first file found.
679	        :param options: the options to be forwarded to the underlying Werkzeug
680	            server. See :func:`werkzeug.serving.run_simple` for more
681	            information.
682	
683	        .. versionchanged:: 1.0
684	            If installed, python-dotenv will be used to load environment
685	            variables from :file:`.env` and :file:`.flaskenv` files.
686	
687	            The :envvar:`FLASK_DEBUG` environment variable will override :attr:`debug`.
688	
689	            Threaded mode is enabled by default.
690	
691	        .. versionchanged:: 0.10
692	            The default port is now picked from the ``SERVER_NAME``
693	            variable.
694	        """
695	        # Ignore this call so that it doesn't start another server if
696	        # the 'flask run' command is used.
697	        if os.environ.get("FLASK_RUN_FROM_CLI") == "true":
698	            if not is_running_from_reloader():
699	                click.secho(
700	                    " * Ignoring a call to 'app.run()' that would block"
701	                    " the current 'flask' CLI command.\n"
702	                    "   Only call 'app.run()' in an 'if __name__ =="
703	                    ' "__main__"\' guard.',
704	                    fg="red",
705	                )
706	
707	            return
708	
709	        if get_load_dotenv(load_dotenv):
710	            cli.load_dotenv()
711	
712	            # if set, env var overrides existing value
713	            if "FLASK_DEBUG" in os.environ:
714	                self.debug = get_debug_flag()
715	
716	        # debug passed to method overrides all other sources
717	        if debug is not None:
718	            self.debug = bool(debug)
719	
720	        server_name = self.config.get("SERVER_NAME")
721	        sn_host = sn_port = None
722	
723	        if server_name:
724	            sn_host, _, sn_port = server_name.partition(":")
725	
726	        if not host:
727	            if sn_host:
728	                host = sn_host
729	            else:
730	                host = "127.0.0.1"
731	
732	        if port or port == 0:
733	            port = int(port)
734	        elif sn_port:
735	            port = int(sn_port)
736	        else:
737	            port = 5000
738	
739	        options.setdefault("use_reloader", self.debug)
740	        options.setdefault("use_debugger", self.debug)
741	        options.setdefault("threaded", True)
742	
743	        cli.show_server_banner(self.debug, self.name)
744	
745	        from werkzeug.serving import run_simple
746	
747	        try:
748	            run_simple(t.cast(str, host), port, self, **options)
749	        finally:
750	            # reset the first request information if the development server
751	            # reset normally.  This makes it possible to restart the server
752	            # without reloader and that stuff from an interactive shell.
753	            self._got_first_request = False

830	    def handle_http_exception(
831	        self, ctx: AppContext, e: HTTPException
832	    ) -> HTTPException | ft.ResponseReturnValue:
833	        """Handles an HTTP exception.  By default this will invoke the
834	        registered error handlers and fall back to returning the
835	        exception as response.
836	
837	        .. versionchanged:: 1.0.3
838	            ``RoutingException``, used internally for actions such as
839	             slash redirects during routing, is not passed to error
840	             handlers.
841	
842	        .. versionchanged:: 1.0
843	            Exceptions are looked up by code *and* by MRO, so
844	            ``HTTPException`` subclasses can be handled with a catch-all
845	            handler for the base ``HTTPException``.
846	
847	        .. versionadded:: 0.3
848	        """
849	        # Proxy exceptions don't have error codes.  We want to always return
850	        # those unchanged as errors
851	        if e.code is None:
852	            return e
853	
854	        # RoutingExceptions are used internally to trigger routing
855	        # actions, such as slash redirects raising RequestRedirect. They
856	        # are not raised or handled in user code.
857	        if isinstance(e, RoutingException):
858	            return e
859	
860	        handler = self._find_error_handler(e, ctx.request.blueprints)
861	        if handler is None:
862	            return e
863	        return self.ensure_sync(handler)(e)  # type: ignore[no-any-return]

866	        self, ctx: AppContext, e: Exception
867	    ) -> HTTPException | ft.ResponseReturnValue:
868	        """This method is called whenever an exception occurs that
869	        should be handled. A special case is :class:`~werkzeug
870	        .exceptions.HTTPException` which is forwarded to the
871	        :meth:`handle_http_exception` method. This function will either
872	        return a response value or reraise the exception with the same
873	        traceback.
874	
875	        .. versionchanged:: 1.0
876	            Key errors raised from request data like ``form`` show the
877	            bad key in debug mode rather than a generic bad request
878	            message.
879	
880	        .. versionadded:: 0.7
881	        """
882	        if isinstance(e, BadRequestKeyError) and (
883	            self.debug or self.config["TRAP_BAD_REQUEST_ERRORS"]
884	        ):
885	            e.show_exception = True
886	
887	        if isinstance(e, HTTPException) and not self.trap_http_exception(e):
888	            return self.handle_http_exception(ctx, e)
889	
890	        handler = self._find_error_handler(e, ctx.request.blueprints)
891	
892	        if handler is None:
893	            raise
894	
895	        return self.ensure_sync(handler)(e)  # type: ignore[no-any-return]

897	    def handle_exception(self, ctx: AppContext, e: Exception) -> Response:
898	        """Handle an exception that did not have an error handler
899	        associated with it, or that was raised from an error handler.
900	        This always causes a 500 ``InternalServerError``.
901	
902	        Always sends the :data:`got_request_exception` signal.
903	
904	        If :data:`PROPAGATE_EXCEPTIONS` is ``True``, such as in debug
905	        mode, the error will be re-raised so that the debugger can
906	        display it. Otherwise, the original exception is logged, and
907	        an :exc:`~werkzeug.exceptions.InternalServerError` is returned.
908	
909	        If an error handler is registered for ``InternalServerError`` or
910	        ``500``, it will be used. For consistency, the handler will
911	        always receive the ``InternalServerError``. The original
912	        unhandled exception is available as ``e.original_exception``.
913	
914	        .. versionchanged:: 1.1.0
915	            Always passes the ``InternalServerError`` instance to the
916	            handler, setting ``original_exception`` to the unhandled
917	            error.
918	
919	        .. versionchanged:: 1.1.0
920	            ``after_request`` functions and other finalization is done
921	            even for the default 500 response when there is no handler.
922	
923	        .. versionadded:: 0.3
924	        """
925	        exc_info = sys.exc_info()
926	        got_request_exception.send(self, _async_wrapper=self.ensure_sync, exception=e)
927	        propagate = self.config["PROPAGATE_EXCEPTIONS"]
928	
929	        if propagate is None:
930	            propagate = self.testing or self.debug
931	
932	        if propagate:
933	            # Re-raise if called with an active exception, otherwise
934	            # raise the passed in exception.
935	            if exc_info[1] is e:
936	                raise
937	
938	            raise e
939	
940	        self.log_exception(ctx, exc_info)
941	        server_error: InternalServerError | ft.ResponseReturnValue
942	        server_error = InternalServerError(original_exception=e)
943	        handler = self._find_error_handler(server_error, ctx.request.blueprints)
944	
945	        if handler is not None:
946	            server_error = self.ensure_sync(handler)(server_error)
947	
948	        return self.finalize_request(ctx, server_error, from_error_handler=True)

966	    def dispatch_request(self, ctx: AppContext) -> ft.ResponseReturnValue:
967	        """Does the request dispatching.  Matches the URL and returns the
968	        return value of the view or error handler.  This does not have to
969	        be a response object.  In order to convert the return value to a
970	        proper response object, call :func:`make_response`.

974	           moved to the new :meth:`full_dispatch_request`.

982	        # request came with the OPTIONS method, reply automatically

985	            and req.method == "OPTIONS"

988	        # otherwise dispatch to the handler for that endpoint
989	        view_args: dict[str, t.Any] = req.view_args  # type: ignore[assignment]

1006	                DeprecationWarning,
